memoryArtifacts.description:
Seymour

The Seymour family (anciently de St. Maur) of Hatch is earliest recorded seated at Penhow Castle in Glamorgan in the 12th century. The parish church of Penhow is dedicated to St Maur. It shou …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Seymour於1349年出生在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘時，他的父親Sir Roger Seymour28歲，母親Cecily de Beauchamp28歲。他與Margaret de Brockbury在英國英格蘭賀福爾可爾瓦爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1391年8月25日在他的家鄉享年42歲。

nameMeanings.name: Seymour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Semar
meanings: Germanized form of French Saint Marc, the name of a Huguenot family that migrated to the Palatinate.
English: variant of   Seymour  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When William Seymour was born in 1349, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, his father, Sir Roger Seymour, was 28 and his mother, Cecily de Beauchamp, was 28. He married Margaret de Brockbury in Colwall, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 25 August 1391, in his hometown, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Seymour est né en 1349, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger Seymour, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Beauchamp, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Margaret de Brockbury à Colwall, Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 25 août 1391, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William Seymour(이)가 1349년 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Roger Seymour(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecily de Beauchamp(은)는 28세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 코월에서 Margaret de Brockbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1391년 8월 25일 그의 고향에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Seymour родился в 1349 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Roger Seymour было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Beauchamp, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Brockbury в местности колвалл, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 25 августа 1391 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 42 лет.
